blurb:
Named after a misunderstood witch who turned out to be the most interesting person in the room — no pressure, Elphaba. She's an adult female shorthair at Good Mews, and her listing doesn't include a full bio, so this is based on her photo and what we know about the shelter.

She looks like a cat with a point of view. The kind who will watch you from across the room before deciding whether you're worth her time, and then absolutely make you feel like you earned it when she does. Good Mews runs cage-free, so she's been living alongside other cats in a home setting. She knows how to share space.

If the name resonated with you even a little, go meet her. Good Mews is on Robinson Road in Marietta. Reach out before you visit so they can set up a proper introduction.
shelter_hours: Please contact shelter for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Wicked-inspired name delivers instant pop-culture resonance that broadens newsletter appeal beyond typical pet adopters
• 'Misunderstood but worth it' narrative arc is emotionally compelling and reader-relatable
• Independent personality profile may narrow her adopter pool, making a feature placement strategically valuable

blurb:
Jane is an adult calico mix, and if you know anything about calicos, you already have a guess about her personality. They've got opinions. They're not shy about them. Jane looks the part too — that patchy tricolor coat and the expression of someone who has seen things and formed conclusions.

Her listing came in pretty bare, so we don't have details on whether she's a lap cat, how she does with other animals, or what her daily vibe is like at home. Worth a direct conversation with Patty's Kitties to fill in the gaps before you go meet her.

Patty's Kitties is based in Atlanta. Submit an inquiry through their Petfinder page to ask about Jane and find out if she'd be a good fit for your place.
shelter_hours: Contact via Petfinder for appointment details
scoring_notes:
• Calico personality archetype gives the blurb strong character voice that resonates with cat-savvy readers
• The 'cat with opinions' framing is witty and memorable — above-average newsletter storytelling potential
• Sparse listing with no time signals; adult calicos are often overlooked, adding quiet urgency for the right audience
